If this gets blocked, I owe you a reset.

tweets.text:
The job that AI was supposed to replace is experiencing the opposite of the expected outcome. Software job postings are outpacing other fields.

Why is that? If you lower the cost of production of something that has lots of use cases, people want more things produced. We’ve seen this play out in the industrial world constantly, and now we’re finally seeing it in knowledge work.

Because software now is much lower to cost per unit, people want way more of it. So we start to use software for all new things and people and companies light up more software projects than ever before. 

But because the job itself is not fully automated (and likely won’t be for as far out as we can see), you still need people that understand these systems to maintain the code, decide what to build, run it over the long run, update it, and more. That all requires people to do work. 

The same thing is going to happen in many other fields as well as we bring down the cost of production of previously extremely scarce areas of work. Agents will cause more abundance than replacement.
tweets.summary_zh:
人工智能本来是要取代某些工作，但现在恰恰相反，软件相关的工作岗位反而在增加。因为软件的生产成本降低后，大家对软件的需求增多了，所以很多公司开始开发更多的软件项目。

尽管一些工作能被自动化，但大部分岗位仍然需要人来维护和管理这些系统。因此，随着生产成本降低，未来会出现更多新的工作机会，而不是取代原有的工作。

tweets.summary_zh:
如果你只在关注人工智能工程的需求时了解了杰文斯悖论（Jevons Paradox，一个经济学理论，指的是当某种资源使用效率提高时，整体需求反而可能增加），那你可能没完全明白它在以下情况下的影响：

- 人类能够熟练使用编程助手（即人工智能工程师）
- 编程助手的应用打破了其他知识工作（如文案、设计等）的限制

随着劳动效率提高，知识工作的单位成本下降，整体的工作需求和对更好知识的需求反而会上升，而不是下降。编码的变化不是个例，而是未来趋势的预兆。

tweets.text:
This is a great post if youre thinking about applied AI in the enterprise. The headline of this post is about what companies have huge upside from AI, but the deepest nuggets are about what AI transformation looks like in an organization.

It’s fundamentally about changing the underlying workflow or business process. As we move from chat tools to agents, those agents actually have to be deployed against workflows, which usually span multiple functions in an org. This is a different way of deploying AI than solely rolling it out to end users. It takes much more work upfront, but the results are the things that actually drive significant ROI. 

“Software asks the employee to adopt a tool, but infrastructure changes the operating layer underneath the employee. The employee should still know what happened, and the process owner should still be able to pause the workflow, change a rule, approve an exception, or pull a person back in when needed. But the value should not depend on someone remembering to use the AI every day.”

The winners of this will be the platforms that can be deployed for specific workflows and business processes with a deep domain expertise. The playbook will often heavily require FDE support, change management, getting data well organized, be able to have comprehensive evals for the workflows, and much more to get right.
tweets.summary_zh:
如果你考虑在企业中应用 AI，这篇文章很值得一读。文章讲的是企业如何通过 AI 转型来改变工作流程，不只是把 AI 使用在员工身上，而是要把它嵌入到整个业务流程中。

简单来说，AI 不只是个工具，它要深入到公司的运作里，帮助员工更好地完成工作。虽然前期准备需要更多的努力，但这样才能带来真正的收益。成功的企业会是那些能够为特定工作流程提供专业 AI 解决方案的平台。

tweets.text:
Thanks to the 1,400+ replies in the first 12 hours! I read all of them last night, and another 300+ came in overnight that I’ll catch up on today. Here’s the Top 10 stack rank from your feedback so far, along with where we stand on each:

1) Make Google Workspace integrations work more reliably: This is the clear #1 request, and all of us agree. On it. A set of improvements went into Gemini Spark recently, but this needs to be much better across the entire app.

2) More reliable tool calling: Strongly agree. We know we need to improve here, and you should expect to see noticeable gains soon.

3) Projects & folder organization for chats: Notebooks have been a positive step, but not enough. We’ll rethink this UX and will report back.

4) Add MCPs and Custom Skills: We’ve rolled out early support in Gemini Spark with custom MCPs, custom skills, and a few connected apps, but we're working to make this more widely available and adding more MCPs.

5) Deep Research improvements: You asked for export reports to NotebookLM and the ability to switch between Deep Research mode and Flash/Pro models in the same chat. I wasn’t aware of these pain points. Both are good ideas, and we’ll figure something out.

6) Remove watermarks from Nano Banana: We’ll chat about this later today to see where we can make adjustments while navigating AI rules, which vary by country.

7) Edit any message in chat history: On it. We’ll aim to do this, plus keep our existing chat branching feature.

8) Improve in-app voice dictation accuracy: We're prioritizing this area overall. We’ll dig into these specific replies, and I might DM a few of you to get more info if we need it.

9) Fix mobile app scrolling bugs: Doing this.

10) Fix "Celebrity Likeness" guardrails: We plan to keep this as-is right now.

Thanks again!

tweets.text:
Some praise and feedback about OpenAI's launches:

1. More than any other lab, OpenAI has the opportunity to make working with agents mainstream. ChatGPT with images, live voice, browser/computer use, and plugins for all your favorite apps is the closest thing to working with a super-capable coworker who can learn and do any white collar work.

2. I didn't get to test GPT 5.6 weeks early, but after a day of testing, I think my best compliment is: "It's got that dog in it." 

It basically never gives up, no matter how complicated a task you give it, and is very reliable thanks to browser use and the other features above. 

It does seem to burn more tokens than GPT 5.5, although maybe I don't have the right settings. I'm using Sol on High.

3.  GPT Live is arguably a more important launch than today's updates for the masses.  

We've been talking to each other using voice for 100K+ years, while the keyboard and mouse weren't introduced until the 1950s/60s. Maybe voice will soon make these two tools look like old computer punch cards. 
 
Also, the best thing about GPT Live is that it lets me pick the intelligence level. I care about latency, but I care more about talking to someone who's smart and gets me.

Speaking of which, it'd be cool if I could have a live conversation with ChatGPT voice and see the tasks it's doing for me.

Ok, now for some constructive feedback:

4. I think the ChatGPT Work vs. Codex thing is confusing. It raises questions like, "So developers aren't working?" and "What if I'm using it to plan my vacation, is that work?" 

I think the whole thing should just be called ChatGPT, Codex, or ChatGPT Codex and there should be no tabs or toggles. IMO, Codex is not that complicated for a normie to understand and use. You're still chatting, except now it can do stuff. 

The sooner this is all unified, the better.

5. I'm very confused about when I should use Sol, Terra, or Luna, and whether I should set effort to Light, Medium, High, etc. 

There's nothing in the UI that walks through what to use when. I understand power users might want these options, but regular people will just get confused.

6. Another thing that's confusing is tasks vs. chat. Ideally, your past chats should still be there in the left nav, except now those chats can do something with plugins. Normies will not understand why their chat history is suddenly harder to find.

7. When I was a PM, I loved building with a community of trusted users who gave me feedback along the way before general availability. But I also tried to make it transparent to users what qualifications were needed to join this community. I don't mind that OpenAI is doing the former, but the latter is not clear at all.

Thanks for listening. Codex has changed how I work and for that I'm very grateful.
tweets.summary_zh:
对OpenAI最近的新功能，有些赞美和反馈：

1. OpenAI有机会让人与智能助手的合作变得普及，就像超级能干的同事一样，ChatGPT现在能处理图像、语音、浏览器操作以及各种应用的插件，非常强大。

2. 虽然我没能提前试用GPT 5，但经过一天的测试，我觉得它非常可靠，从不轻易放弃任务。可能消耗的资源比GPT 5.5多，但我可能还没调到最佳设置。

3. GPT Live的推出可能比今天的更新更重要。我们用语音交流了超过10万年，而键盘和鼠标才在上世纪50、60年代出现。将来，语音可能会让那些工具显得过时。

4. 关于ChatGPT和Codex的区分让人困惑。不知道开发者是不是在“工作”，还有比如我用它

tweets.text:
Great post on some of the dynamics to think through for the future competitive advantage in world when AI models are shared amongst firms and packing so much for the intelligence of that industry.

This is going to become a core question for companies and the economy broadly over the next decade and beyond. If AI is trained on the best datasets in every single industry - like law, finance, healthcare, or life sciences  - then how do you compete and differentiate in the future?

This is a great open question that I don’t think is perfectly knowable right now because of how fast AI progress is happening. But ultimately it stands to reason that if intelligence is abundant and broadly available to anyone in a field, then the companies that effectively use it the best and against a set of data and knowledge that grows in value over time, will be in a strong position. 

There’s a huge reinforcing loop between the intelligence from models, a company’s own data, the connection of that data and AI in their workflows, and how employees ultimately interact with that system to create value. There’s no obvious point where this will become uniform across all companies in a vertical because each company will approach this in a different way, just as they already do with their talent and workflows. If anything, there will be compounding returns to those that do this best that accelerate their advantage over time.

Overall, super interesting question to see how this plays out over time.
tweets.summary_zh: 如果各公司都能使用共享的人工智能（AI）模型，未来它们该如何竞争和区分自己？在接下来的十年，利用好AI的公司将处于有利位置，因为聪明的做法和企业数据的结合将创造价值。每家公司都会有自己的方式来使用AI和数据，这意味着那些能最好地整合这些资源的公司将获得更多的优势。这个话题非常有趣，值得关注未来的发展。

tweets.text:
GPT-5.6 is now out. We've been evaluating the model family on the Box AI Complex Work eval, which tests the model with the Box AI Agent on a variety of extremely hard tasks using enterprise document sets. 

Sol is a big step up from GPT-5.5, especially on complex data-oriented tasks that require deep reasoning and analysis, and the gains concentrate exactly where enterprise work is hardest. Here are a few examples that we saw across our tests:

* Financial Services (76% vs 71%): On a multi-year projection, Sol anchored to the correct opening balance sheet date rather than assuming a clean January 1 start, then carried revenue, earnings, and interest through to the right figures year over year where one early wrong assumption compounds through every downstream cell.

* Healthcare (58% vs 46%): On a critical-care case review, Sol identified the correct diagnosis and intervention and avoided the dangerous misstep of ordering imaging before the time-critical procedure, a trap GPT-5.5 walked into.

* Public Sector (74% vs 63%): Handed a class's raw gradebook and a new grading directive, Sol mapped each assignment to the right weight bucket, treating homework as zero-weight practice per the directive. It recomputed every student's grade to within a tenth of a percent, where GPT-5.5 drifted partway through.

* Life Sciences (60% vs 51%): Across four separate compound datasets, Sol intersected the ranked target lists exactly (case-sensitive, no shortcuts) to find the biological targets common to all four, catching the shared targets GPT-5.5 missed.

Sol reasons from the source definitions and checks the documents rather than taking them at face value and it's most reliable exactly where the numbers drive real decisions. This will be huge for enterprise agents using unstructured enterprise data.

GPT-5.6 will be available to customers shortly within the Box AI Studio for building custom agents with.
tweets.summary_zh: GPT-5.6新版本上线啦！这个新模型在处理复杂数据和分析任务时表现得更加出色，特别是在企业工作最难的地方，比如财务、医疗和公共部门。它能更准确地理解和处理文件信息，帮助企业在关键决策时做到更准确，能够对客户迅速提供帮助，提升工作效率。很快，大家就可以在Box AI Studio中使用这个新模型，来打造自己的智能助手。

tweets.text:
Me explaining this week’s model release situation to my wife..

"babe listen. this matters. GPT-5.6 dropped in three flavors. Sol, Terra, and Luna. Sol is the smart one. It had to clear government safety evals before release, like getting paroled. Luna is the cheap one. Terra exists.

Grok 4.5 launched the day BEFORE GPT-5.6. On purpose. So it could have 24 hours of being frontier-adjacent. Elon calls it Opus-class, which is a polite way of saying it competes with Anthropic's previous model. But it's $2 per million tokens, so nobody will care.

Speaking of Anthropic. Fable 5 just came back online after the government suspended it for three weeks. The best coding model on earth was literally grounded. It's back now, and the rumored GPT-6 is being retrained specifically to beat it.

Sonnet 5 also shipped last week. Near-Opus performance at $2 per million. It's the new free default. Meaning the free tier is now better than what enterprises paid six figures for in January.

Meituan open sourced LongCat-2.0. That's a 1.6 TRILLION parameter model. MIT licensed. From a Chinese FOOD DELIVERY company. Your DoorDash equivalent is now a frontier lab. Let that one sit.

ByteDance launched Seedream 5 Pro. Almost as good as GPT Image 2 at 4x cheaper, because ByteDance's entire corporate strategy is 'same thing but cheaper.' It beat Meta's new Muse Image, which trains on your public Instagram photos unless you opt out. Your 2014 gym mirror selfie is now foundational research.

OpenAI also shipped GPT-Live, a voice model that goes 'mhmm' and 'got it' WHILE you're still talking. We finally taught AI to pretend to listen. Full parity with husbands achieved.

And Ollama raised $65M so you can run the open source versions of all of this on your own laptop, which is great for privacy and also heating your apartment.

And btw it’s just Thursday"
tweets.summary_zh:
这周的 AI 世界真热闹，来了好多新产品！GPT-5.6 有三个版本，其中 Sol 是最聪明的。还有 Grok 4.5 也刚发布，故意和 GPT-5.6 竞争。Antropic 的 Fable 5 经过暂停后也回归了，大家都在争着做更好的编程模型。

另外，Meituan 开源了超强的 LongCat-2.0，以前你吃外卖的地方，现在变成了高科技实验室；而 ByteDance 推出的 Seedream 5 Pro 性能媲美大牌，但价格便宜四倍。最特别的是，OpenAI 还推出了一个可以实时回应你说话的语音模型，听起来就像在认真听你说话一样！

tweets.text:
New in Claude Code: /checkup

Run /checkup to:

1. Clean up unused skills/MCPs/plugins and save context
2. Dedup your local CLAUDE.md against the checked in CLAUDE.md
3. Break up root CLAUDE.md into nested CLAUDE.md's + skills
4. Turn off slow hooks
5. Update your Claude Code to the latest version
6. Enable auto mode by default
7. Pre-approve frequently denied read-only commands

.. And a few other goodies.

/checkup confirms with you before making any changes. Enjoy!
tweets.summary_zh: Claude Code 更新了一个新功能叫 /checkup，使用它可以清理不需要的技能和插件，整理文件，还能自动更新到最新版本哦。这就像给你的聊天机器人做一次健康检查，确保它运转良好，不会慢半拍！在做任何更改之前，它会先确认一下，让你放心使用。

tweets.text:
true. let’s unpack this..people think data and evals are low skill, grunt work that you can just buy and stuff into models. 

in practice, the model lifecycle looks something like model strategy -> evals -> pre/post training/rl aligned to evals -> GTM.

like any product strategy, you need strong opinions up front but it is 100x harder because of the allure of building an LLM that is great at everything. 

the next challenge is staying focused on the target eval sets throughout the model build through challenges with the latest architecture, regressions, competing data contributions and the latest competitor news.

you then make choices between multiple checkpoints trading off on target evals, regression evals and early customer feedback.

so yes, it begins with an opinion expressed through evals and if you execute well, you land somewhere near your target.
tweets.summary_zh: 很多人认为处理数据和评估模型很简单，可以随便买来做，但实际上，模型的开发过程非常复杂。你需要从一开始就有清晰的想法，然后在整个过程中，注意各种挑战和反馈，才能最终取得好的结果。总之，成功并不是偶然，而是需要认真规划和执行的。

tweets.text:
Just coming off of meetings with a couple dozen enterprise IT leaders discussing AI agents. Here are a few of the common themes that stand out:

* Lots of conversation that you have to solve an operating model challenge to get the full benefits of AI. Most companies have orgs that have always operated in siloes; but agents are most effectively when they are tied to a process, which often cuts across these siloes. So the big question is how do you start to deploy centrally managed agents that can work across organizational boundaries. Who manages these agents? How do they get deployed and adopted? 

* Data fragmentation remains a major issue for most organizations. As long as data remains highly fragmented and not in standard formats, or data is not available to the right people and agents, enterprises are dealing with issues around being able to get answers from agents that are accurate or that conform to their business practices. This cuts across both systems with structured data (product metrics or revenue figures) and unstructured data (product roadmap or customer contracts).

* Clear sense that companies need to figure out what their core data moats are going to be in the future. If everyone has access to roughly the same superintelligence from the various models, then the context that you feed the models becomes proprietary value in the future. Capturing this data and getting it into a format that agents can use becomes very important.

* Everyone is trying to figure out the right metrics to manage to for AI adoption. General consensus that tokens are not the right metric per se, and people leaning more toward business outcomes (in an ideal world). For business outcomes (like more revenue or more shipped product), though, you have to get close to each individual workflow to figure out if it was successfully transformed with AI so it’s harder to manage top down.

* Growing view that enterprises are going to live in a multi-model world. Lots of interest (though early in actual adoption) in layers that can route workloads to different models (frontside or open weights) for cost or performance reasons. Also enterprises are trying to figure out what things do you give to the models directly vs. what do you separate as horizontal systems and context so you can swap any system in and out. 

* Talent for driving AI adoption and implementation still remains a major issue and topic. Many view it as something you necessarily have to train for internally due to a shortage of talent being trained on this in the outside. As an aside, this feels like it remains a huge opportunity for those that get very good at deploying and management agents in an enterprise since most companies are looking for these skills.

* The best use-cases for AI tend to be those that fundamentally change the work being done instead of just replacing an existing process and doing it more efficiently. Companies are working through their versions of this individually because it’s different per industry, but this often remains both the most exciting and higher upside uses of AI.

Many more topics discussed recently, but overall it’s clear that there’s a ton of change going on with much more to come.

最近和很多企业 IT 领导开会聊 AI 应用，发现了一些共同点：

很多公司在使用 AI 时面临组织结构问题，如何让 AI 在不同部门之间有效运作是个大挑战。另外，数据分散成了主要问题，数据格式不统一使得 AI 难以提供准确的答案。企业还需要明确未来的核心数据价值，因为未来的 AI 模型会竞争相似的智能，而上下文数据将变得非常重要。

人才短缺仍然是个大问题，内部培训成了必需，许多公司需要掌握 AI 部署和管理

tweets.text:
A small percentage of useful data is on the open web available to all models for training or for agents to operate with. Most of it lives inside of organizations and often is in legacy systems, in people’s heads, or is fragmented across the enterprise. 

It’s the marketing plans, product roadmaps, development practices, contracts, financial data, strategies, and general corporate knowledge that every company operates off of. 

Whether it’s for training a model or used as context for agents, this data will increasingly be more valuable over time. The ability to get the right data to agents to operate on, securely, will be a defining characteristic of how companies operate and compete in the future. 

Effective use of AI will in the economy will largely come down to the companies that are able to have agents best understand their business and make the right decisions on their behalf. Data actually is the new oil.

tweets.text:
Fable 5 will leave Claude subscriptions tomorrow at midnight. Here are 5 use cases worth trying before then, with prompts you can copy and paste:

1) FIND FABLE-WORTHY WORK
You're Fable 5, the most capable model available. Look through my projects, docs, and your memory, and list the top 5 tasks genuinely worth running on you. Rank them with a one-line reason each. Don't do the work yet.

2) GET LIFE & BUSINESS ADVICE
You're my business advisor. Read my plan doc and pull live data from my connected tools. Write a one-page assessment of my business and the top 3 things to focus on for the next 3 months, plus what to drop and why.

3) MAKE YOUR PROJECT SHIP-READY
I'm about to ship this project. Find everything wrong with it first. Read the whole codebase and hunt for real bugs, broken edge cases, and anything that'll break in front of a user. List each issue with how to reproduce it and the fix. Hold a high bar.

4) PLAN THE NEXT BIG THING
I want to plan a big project: [describe it]. Don't build yet. Lay out the full plan: phases, key decisions, risks, and open questions. Flag anything that could sink it, and make it clear enough that a cheaper model could execute step by step.

5) REFACTOR YOUR AI SKILLS
Refactor my AI skill system. Read all my skills, map where they overlap or conflict, then clean them up: tighten workflows, cut bloat, and standardize structure. Keep each skill working and check in at major milestones.

📌 Watch my tutorial for a live demo of all 5 use cases: https://t.co/XElMEV3FwK

The ultimate test for coding AI: is software as a whole getting better? Are companies shipping faster? Do you now have apps and games you couldn't even dream of before? Is the software you're getting bug-free?

For me: my own ability to influence my productivity has radically increased. I feel a level of agency and autonomy I never had before. I'm free to create and execute. I can communicate a vision with my colleagues instantly. Personal software is real and a big win, I have a bunch of web tools and macOS apps I wrote just for myself.

For Vercel: it's been amazing to see our CTO @cramforce bring novel software to life with swaths of agents. Genuinely useful software that's having an impact industry-wide emerged nearly autonomously (https://t.co/zwHPbMHK45, https://t.co/TTbSu13EjV, https://t.co/HedSpZpVQI). That's just an example. Our PM team ships software and fixes bugs. Our junior engineers and interns ship quality software at scale. On a personal note, AI at my fingertips, as CLI or v0, allows me to keep up with all the product updates. I can build demos, apps and benchmarks on top of @vercel as new products go out.

But the ultimate jury is that people should love our products, expand their usage, refer their friends and colleagues to the platform. So far that tracks well, and it's the main metric I watch (and obviously we take token costs seriously, just like any other infrastructure spend… there isn't and has never been a proclamation to "tokenmaxxx").

tweets.text:
Great post on how to think about open source AI and the applied AI layer. Two things will always be true in AI. Frontier intelligence will likely remain at the forefront of solving brand new used cases and often be used for orchestrating and planning of any type of complex workflow. 

At the same time, as use-cases become mature and predictable in an enterprise context, you can begin to peel off some set of the tokens to either lower cost open or closed models *or* models that are trained for the task at hand. 

Doing this too early in the adoption curve of any new use-case doesn’t make sense as you don’t know what you’re optimizing for, which is why there will always be a bit of a lag here.

This process can essentially run on forever as there is no end for both the benefits of frontier intelligence or tuned models. This is why the spend and token volume for both approaches will continue to go up for years to come as we’re still early on both.

Again, the only reason this dynamic is possible though is because of the applied AI layer that can effectively eval their workflows in a specific domain, choose a mixture of models to solve tasks, and have enough scale to eventually train their own models for their purposes.
tweets.summary_zh:
这篇文章讨论了开源人工智能和实际应用层的重要性。简单说，尖端的人工智能（指最新、最强的技术）在解决新问题时会很有效，而随着企业应用案例变得成熟，企业可以选择更便宜的模型来降低成本。但如果在新应用开始时就赶着选择便宜的方案，可能会不太合理，因为还不清楚最需要优化什么。

这个过程就像无尽的循环，因为尖端人工智能和专门训练的模型的优势是永远存在的，未来几年相关的支出和使用量还会继续增长。不过，这一切都得益于能够在特定领域评估工作流程的实际应用层，帮助企业选择合适的模型并最终训练出适合自己的模型。

tweets.text:
VCs: this job is investing in outliers. 

Also VC: here’s our revolutionary sourcing algorithm, custom built by our in house team, RLed on decades of data, with proprietary data signals from the moment someone is born. 

This job is very weird where there is so much serendipity and luck involved in how you stumbled upon an investment. However, it does take a lot of conviction and if you have years of bets where you have been right, then you aren’t simply “lucky”. 

Returns are the only alpha.. but those take time and so we tend to resort to random metrics to prove how we’re different from the next VC. 

The unscientific way I have tried to play this game is by massively expanding my surface of luck to try to find these outliers generally and having a prepared mind for 3-4 areas that are 6-12 months early where I can meet every entrepreneur. 

Time will only tell if this works!

tweets.text:
A silly, but made me laugh interaction with Sol

-----------------

🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡
🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡
🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡

> 🫡 Salute received, loud and clear. Carry on!

* 12

> 39 × 12 = 468 🫡

- 800

> 468 − 800 = −332 🫡

?

> There were 39 🫡 emojis.
> (39 × 12) − 800 = 468 − 800 = −332.

how can you have negative emojis

> You can’t physically. −332 🫡 means you’re short 332 salutes or ... owe 332 salutes.
> Actual emojis remaining: 0 🫡, with a 332-salute debt.

🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡
🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡
🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡🫡

> Payment received: 39 🫡
> Remaining salute debt: 293 🫡

hahaha

> The salute economy is ruthless. 🫡📉
tweets.summary_zh: 这段对话很搞笑，讲的是用“敬礼”表情（🫡）来计算与“敬礼债务”的概念。就是说有39个敬礼，但在计算中却发现自己欠了332个敬礼，增添了些幽默感。简单来说，就是在调侃一个虚构的“表情符号经济”，让人忍俊不禁。

The best way to catch bugs is to use the product and see if you can break it. 

You’re not going to find most bugs by dead reckoning the code. 

You’re reviewing the code itself for architecture and API design… to control the increase in technical debt

tweets.text:
One of the things I love about Claude Fable 5 is that it knew to use propensity score matching (matching users on activity so you compare like with like) in my retention analysis without me asking.

It’s exciting to see Fable 5’s improved judgment across all of its work, from writing emails and docs in Cowork to debugging complex errors in Claude Code
tweets.summary_zh: 我很喜欢 Claude Fable 5，因为它在我分析用户留存（用户继续使用产品的情况）时，主动用了一个叫“倾向得分匹配”（简单来说，就是根据用户的活动把相似的用户放在一起比较）的方法，而我并没有特别要求它这么做。看到 Fable 5 在写邮件、文档和调试复杂错误方面都提升了判断力，真的让我很兴奋！

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: Why AI Agents Don't Actually Understand You — Danielle Perszyk, Amazon AGI Lab
podcasts.summary_zh:
视频探讨了人工智能代理为何不能真正理解人类的思维和情感。演讲者 Danielle Perszyk，来自亚马逊 AGI 实验室，分析了 AI 如何在表面上模拟理解，但实际上缺乏深层次的情感和认知能力。

💡 值得一看 — 这个视频可以帮助 Yves 更好地理解 AI 技术的局限性，以便在 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 的创意设计中，避免过度依赖 AI 的假象理解。

podcasts.title: Why AI Agents Make Microtransactions Viable #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了人工智能代理如何使微交易变得可行，分析了AI技术在处理小额支付时的优势和应用前景。参与者分享了关于用户体验和商业模式的见解，指出了未来可能的创新方向。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个话题与 Logic Labs 在AI工具开发的前沿探索密切相关，同时还涉及到在电商运营中应用微交易的潜力。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个话题相关于 Logic Labs 的 AI 工具开发和 TexTale 的品牌内容创作，可以为你在品牌营销和创意设计方面提供深刻的见解。

这个视频中，Anthony Kaldellis 和 Lex Fridman 探讨了罗马帝国是否因瘟疫而失去了约一半的人口，以及这对历史的影响。他们分析了古代社会的结构、瘟疫的传播以及对文化和经济的长期影响，为我们理解历史上的重大事件提供了深刻的视角。

💡 值得一看 — 虽然视频主要讨论历史，但对古代社会结构和危机反应的分析可以启发你在 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 中应对现代市场的挑战和变革。

podcasts.name: TBPN
podcasts.title: China Considers AI Export Controls, SK Hynix $28B IPO, Banks Target Payment Business | Diet TBPN
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了中国在考虑对人工智能（AI）出口进行管控的背景，以及SK海力士（SK Hynix）计划进行280亿美元首次公开募股（IPO）的消息。此外，还有一些关于银行如何瞄准支付业务的趋势分析。

💡 值得一看 — 视频涉及AI出口管控的政策动向，对于理解行业趋势和市场环境非常重要，可能对Logic Labs的业务发展产生影响。

podcasts.name: TBPN
podcasts.title: The only correct blazer technique.
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讲述了如何选择和搭配西装外套的正确技巧，强调细节与整体风格的协调。通过具体的示范，展示了不同场合下西装外套的搭配方式以及应注意的关键要素，让观众能轻松掌握穿着技巧。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频可以为 TexTale 的服装设计提供灵感，同时帮助提升品牌的男士搭配方案，适合对创意设计感兴趣的创业者。

podcasts.name: TBPN
podcasts.title: Hope the former football coach got some equity.
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了一位前足球教练在职场中获得股权的问题，探讨了如何在团队中创造公平和价值。视频中还涉及了领导力和团队建设的重要性，适合希望提升组织效能的人士观看。

💡 值得一看 — 虽然视频内容不是直接与 AI 相关，但关于团队管理的洞察与 Yves 在 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 的创业经验息息相关，有助于丰富创意设计和品牌营销的思路。

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.title: The Big Ways AI Just Changed
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频探讨了人工智能在多个领域带来的重大变化，包括生产效率、营销策略及设计流程的革新。它介绍了如何利用AI技术帮助企业提高创意和运营效率，以及这些变化如何影响未来的发展方向。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 该视频内容与Logic Labs的AI产品开发及TexTale的电商运营紧密相关，能为你的创意设计与品牌营销提供有价值的洞察。

podcasts.name: TBPN
podcasts.title: Alex Hormozi in the Ultradome, China Export Controls, SK Hynix, Big Bank Comeback
podcasts.summary_zh:
本视频讨论了中国的出口管制对全球科技产业的影响，重点分析了SK Hynix及其在半导体产业中的角色，同时探讨了大型银行如何在经济不确定性中重新崛起，借助创新和灵活策略应对挑战。

💡 值得一看 — 这个视频提供了对科技产业最新动态的深刻见解，可能会对Logic Labs和TexTale在AI和电商领域的战略方向有所启发。

podcasts.name: TBPN
podcasts.title: Alex Hormozi's Playbook for Building Billion-Dollar Businesses
podcasts.summary_zh:
在这个视频中，Alex Hormozi分享了他在创建亿万级企业过程中的核心策略和经验，强调了如何确保业务的可持续增长和成功。他讲述了一些实用的商业原则，帮助创业者在竞争激烈的市场中脱颖而出，并有效利用资源。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频中分享的商业策略和经验对于Logic Labs和TexTale的创业过程极具指导意义，特别是在利用AI提高效率和推动创意设计方面。

podcasts.name: Matt Wolfe
podcasts.title: Persian Empire vs Roman Empire: The war that destroyed both empires | Anthony Kaldellis
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了波斯帝国与罗马帝国之间的战争，以及这场冲突如何摧毁了双方的帝国。通过深入的历史分析，嘉宾Anthony Kaldellis探讨了战争的根本原因及其深远影响，揭示了文明的兴衰背后的复杂性。

💡 值得一看 — 本视频提供了对古代文明战争的深刻见解，能够激发对品牌营销和电商运营中历史影响力的思考，对创意设计也有启发。

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.title: AI Focused Companies Are Hiring More
podcasts.summary_zh:
这段视频讨论了专注于人工智能的公司正在积极招聘，分析了行业现状以及未来雇佣趋势的原因。视频强调了AI技能在当前就业市场中的重要性，说明了各类公司在寻找具备AI相关能力的人才。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这与 Logic Labs 在AI领域的探索及TexTale对创意设计和电商运营人才的需求密切相关，有助于理解招聘动态对行业的影响。

podcasts.name: Peter Yang
podcasts.title: Codex helps me manage my meetings
podcasts.summary_zh:
该视频讨论了Codex如何帮助用户有效管理会议，提高效率。通过展示具体的使用案例，视频让观众了解到这一AI工具如何在日常工作中解决时间管理的问题。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频展示了Codex在项目管理中的实际应用，可能为Logic Labs的AI开发和TexTale的电商运营提供启示。

podcasts.name: No Priors
podcasts.title: Priscilla Chan: There are Diseases Nobody is Trying to Cure
podcasts.summary_zh:
视频讨论了普莉西拉·陈（Priscilla Chan）关于未被重视的疾病及其潜在的影响，她强调许多疾病虽然严重，但却缺乏足够的研究和治疗支持。她呼吁人们关注这些被忽视的健康问题，尤其是在科技与医疗交融的时代，AI和创新能够为这些领域带来新的解决方案。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频探讨了AI在医疗领域的应用和潜力，可能为Logic Labs和TexTale在跨界设计上提供新的灵感和思路。

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: The Physics of 4-Bit AI Quantization #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频探讨了4-bit AI量化的物理原理，讲解了如何通过减少模型的位数来提高计算效率和减少存储需求。这一技术在AI模型的优化和部署中非常关键，有助于提升性能和降低成本。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频与AI工具开发密切相关，能为Logic Labs提供量化技术的最新洞察，帮助提升AI产品的性能和应用效果。

podcasts.name: Matt Wolfe
podcasts.title: Why the Roman Empire collapsed in the East | Anthony Kaldellis and Lex Fridman
podcasts.summary_zh:
视频讨论了罗马帝国在东部的崩溃原因，Anthony Kaldellis 和 Lex Fridman 深入探讨了历史因素、政治决策和军事策略如何共同导致这一伟大文明的衰落。通过生动的案例分析，他们揭示了这些历史事件如何塑造了今日的世界。

💡 值得一看 — 这个话题可以启发 Yves 反思品牌和产品发展的历史背景，从中汲取对现代创意设计和电商运营的洞察。

podcasts.name: Training Data
podcasts.title: Inside Zipline's Autonomous System: 140M Miles, Zero Incidents
podcasts.summary_zh:
视频讲述了Zipline的自主系统如何在140百万英里的飞行中实现零事件，展示了其先进的无人机物流技术及有效的运营能力。通过精确的技术和系统优化，Zipline在向偏远地区提供医疗物资方面展现了巨大的潜力和安全性。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频展示了先进的自动化系统，可能为Logic Labs在AI工具和产品开发方面提供启示，同时也有助于TexTale探索利用科技提升电商运营的策略。

podcasts.name: Matt Wolfe
podcasts.title: Lessons from the Roman Empire for modern-day - historian explains | Anthony Kaldellis
podcasts.summary_zh:
视频探讨了古罗马帝国的历史教训，以及这些教训如何能够为当今社会和现代企业提供启发。历史学家安东尼·卡尔德利斯分享了罗马在管理、领导力和创新方面的经验，这些经验在今天依然适用。

💡 值得一看 — 罗马帝国的教训可以启发你在Logic Labs和TexTale中的创新思维，特别是在品牌营销和电商运营方面。

podcasts.name: Matt Wolfe
podcasts.title: The enemies of the Roman Empire who almost destroyed it | Anthony Kaldellis and Lex Fridman
podcasts.summary_zh:
这段视频由Anthony Kaldellis与Lex Fridman探讨了罗马帝国的敌人，他们在历史上几乎摧毁了这个强大的帝国。视频深入分析了这些敌对势力的策略、影响，以及其对罗马的长期影响，展现了一段鲜为人知的历史。

💡 值得一看 — 本视频对历史的深度解析有助于理解文化与品牌如何在竞争激烈的环境中生存与发展，这对Logic Labs和TexTale在调研市场趋势时可能提供启发。

producthunt.name: Acti
producthunt.tagline: Agentic keyboard for mobile commands and search
producthunt.summary_zh: Acti是一款专为移动设备设计的智能键盘，能够快速输入命令和进行搜索。它简化了用户在手机上执行操作的过程，提高了效率。用户可以通过触击键盘，轻松调用各种功能和信息。

producthunt.name: Tencent EdgeOne Makers
producthunt.tagline: Ship AI agents like web apps, in minutes.
producthunt.summary_zh: Tencent EdgeOne Makers 是一款工具，允许用户快速创建和部署 AI 代理，就像使用网页应用一样简单。用户可以在几分钟内开发出具备智能交互功能的应用程序，提升工作效率。该产品支持多种场景，适合不同的应用需求。

producthunt.name: Context.dev
producthunt.tagline: One API to scrape, enrich, and extract the internet
producthunt.summary_zh: Context.dev 是一个API工具，能够从互联网上抓取、丰富和提取数据。用户可以通过它轻松获取网站上的信息，并将这些数据进行整理和分析。它适用于各种数据集成和自动化需求。

producthunt.name: Goldfish
producthunt.tagline: Press Option. It knows your work and replies like you
producthunt.summary_zh: Goldfish是一款智能助手工具，通过按下选项按钮，它可以根据你的工作习惯和风格，自动生成回复信息。无论是邮件、消息还是文档，它都能像你一样进行智能回应，提升你的工作效率。

producthunt.name: Upstream
producthunt.tagline: The inbox designed for humans and agents
producthunt.summary_zh: Upstream是一款专为用户和客服人员设计的收件箱工具。它通过智能整理消息，让信息更清晰，帮助用户和代理快速找到重要内容，提升沟通效率。

producthunt.name: AnySearch
producthunt.tagline: Real-time structured search trusted by agents and developers
producthunt.summary_zh: AnySearch是一款实时的结构化搜索工具，专为代理和开发者设计。它能快速整理和提供准确的信息，让用户能够高效地找到所需的数据和答案。通过直观的界面，AnySearch简化了搜索过程，提高了工作效率。

producthunt.name: ExploreYC
producthunt.tagline: Open-source API for Y Combinator & a16z company data
producthunt.summary_zh: ExploreYC 是一个开源API，提供Y Combinator和a16z公司的数据。用户可以通过这个API获取相关公司的信息、融资情况和其他关键数据，方便进行研究和分析。

producthunt.name: Fypro
producthunt.tagline: Convert your TikTok followers into paying customers
producthunt.summary_zh: Fypro 是一款工具，可以帮助用户将 TikTok 上的粉丝转化为付费客户。它提供数据分析和营销功能，使用户能够有效地推广产品和服务，从而实现收益增长。

producthunt.name: BrowserAct
producthunt.tagline: Web browser automation for AI agents
producthunt.summary_zh: BrowserAct是一款用于Web浏览器自动化的工具，专为人工智能代理设计。它可以自动执行网站上的任务，帮助用户快速完成重复性的网络操作，提高效率。通过简单的设置，用户可以轻松实现自动化的浏览体验。

producthunt.name: Auriko
producthunt.summary_zh: Auriko是一款针对大型语言模型（LLM）的交易平台，用户可以便捷地进行各种API调用和数据交互。它提供了简化的界面，帮助用户高效管理和执行相关的交易任务。通过Auriko，用户可以更轻松地利用语言模型的功能。

producthunt.name: ChatCut
producthunt.tagline: Your AI video editor in ChatGPT, desktop, and web
producthunt.summary_zh: ChatCut是一款智能视频编辑工具，集成在ChatGPT、桌面和网页上。它可以自动剪辑、制作和编辑视频，用户只需输入指令，即可轻松创建专业效果的视频。

producthunt.name: Bluerails Discovery
producthunt.tagline: The rails AI agents use to find and pay you
producthunt.summary_zh: Bluerails Discovery 是一款智能代理工具，能够自动分析和发现合适的机会来为用户赚取收入。它利用人工智能技术，帮助用户快速找到并支付相关资源，简化了收入获取的过程。

producthunt.name: Framer 3.0
producthunt.tagline: With Agents, Branching, Community, and an all-new design
producthunt.summary_zh: Framer 3.0是一款全新的设计和原型制作工具，新增了智能代理、分支功能和社区支持。用户可以轻松创建互动原型，进行项目协作，并与其他用户分享和交流设计思路。它的设计界面简洁易用，适合快速实现创意。

producthunt.name: AgentX
producthunt.tagline: Evaluate AI agent, pinpoint issues, and fix with one click.
producthunt.summary_zh: AgentX 是一款智能工具，可以评估人工智能代理的表现，快速识别问题，并通过一键修复功能解决这些问题。它旨在简化 AI 系统的管理和优化过程。

producthunt.name: Glaze by Raycast
producthunt.tagline: Create your own Mac apps by chatting with AI
producthunt.summary_zh: Glaze by Raycast 是一款让用户通过与人工智能对话来创建自己的 Mac 应用程序的工具。用户可以简单输入需求，AI 会帮你生成相应的应用，实现个性化定制。它使得开发应用变得更加简单快捷，无需编程基础。

github_trending.description: The Destructive Command Guard (dcg) is for blocking dangerous git and shell commands from being executed by agents.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目的主要目的是阻止一些危险的 Git 和 shell 命令被意外执行，从而保护代码和系统的安全。对于 Yves 来说，这个工具在 Logic Labs（AI 公司）的研发过程中能够帮助团队提高安全性，防止潜在的错误和损失，同时也能在 TexTale（科技服装电商）的技术运营中增加安全保障，维护业务的稳定性。作为创意设计从业者，了解这种安全工具的存在，可以激发他对安全设计的思考，以确保创意实现的过程中不被技术问题所影响。

github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个为 Claude 设计的服务，让它能够控制终端、搜索文件系统并编辑文件。对于 Yves 来说，这个项目对 Logic Labs（AI 公司）会更有帮助，因为它可以提升 AI 在操作和管理文件方面的能力，增强 AI 技术的应用效果。同时，这样的工具对于创意设计的工作流程也能提供便利，尤其是在需要处理和管理大量文件和数据时。
这个项目名为Vibe-Trading，是一个个人交易助手，利用人工智能帮助用户进行金融市场的交易决策。对Yves来说，这个项目可能对Logic Labs（AI公司）更有帮助，通过学习和应用其中的AI模型与算法，可以提升公司在智能交易领域的研究和开发能力。同时，作为创意设计从业者，他也可以借鉴这种智能化工具的设计思路，应用于电商或品牌营销的相关创意项目中。

github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个工作流管理框架，用于在 Python 中构建强健的数据处理流程，帮助用户自动化和监控数据任务。对于Yves来说，这个项目对Logic Labs（AI 公司）会更有帮助，因为它能够帮助公司更高效地处理数据，提高AI模型的训练和部署效率。同时，作为创意设计从业者，他也可以借助这些数据处理流程来分析用户行为，优化设计决策。

github_trending.description: 100+ AI Agent & RAG apps you can actually run — clone, customize, ship.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目汇集了超过100个可以实际运行的AI代理和检索增强生成（RAG）应用，用户可以轻松克隆、定制和部署它们。这对于Yves的Logic Labs（AI公司）非常有帮助，因为他们可以利用这些现成的AI工具进行创新开发，同时提升公司在AI领域的竞争力。此外，这些应用也能为TexTale的电商运营提供新的创意解决方案，改善用户体验。

github_trending.description: A collection of notebooks/recipes showcasing some fun and effective ways of using Claude.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个集合，展示了如何有趣且有效地使用 Claude 这个 AI 工具的各种示例和技巧，主要通过 Jupyter Notebook 的形式呈现。对于 Yves 来说，Logic Labs 作为 AI 公司可以从中获得灵感，帮助团队更好地利用 Claude 进行创意设计和开发，而 TexTale 则可以借鉴其中的 AI 应用案例，提升电商运营和品牌营销的创新性。作为创意设计从业者，他也能通过这些示例获得新的思路，探索 AI 在设计领域的多种可能性。

github_trending.description: 🏡 Open source home automation that puts local control and privacy first.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个开源的家庭自动化平台，用户可以通过它控制家中的各种设备，并且重视本地控制和隐私保护。对Yves来说，这个项目可以帮助TexTale（科技服装电商）提升智能家居产品的集成和用户体验，同时也为创意设计提供灵感，探索如何将智能科技融入现代生活。

github_trending.description: Project N.O.M.A.D, is a self-contained, offline survival computer packed with critical tools, knowledge, and AI to keep you informed and empowered—anytime, anywhere.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个自给自足的离线求生计算机，内置关键工具和知识，用于在任何时间和地点为用户提供信息和支持。对于Yves来说，这个项目可能对Logic Labs（AI公司）更有帮助，因为它展示了如何将AI应用于实际场景，提升用户体验和自我管理能力，同时也能激发创意设计的灵感。

github_trending.description: An open-source background agents coding system
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个开源的后台代理系统，可以帮助开发者更高效地处理后台任务和服务。对于Yves来说，这个项目可能对Logic Labs（AI公司）更有帮助，因为它可以提升其AI应用的后台处理能力，进而优化产品性能和用户体验。同时，作为一个创意设计从业者，Yves也可以借鉴该项目的系统架构和代码组织方式，提升其在AI和设计结合方面的开发效率。

github_trending.description: Advanced UX and interoperability extension for Wand (WeMod) app
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个为 Wand（WeMod 应用）提供高级用户体验和互操作性扩展的工具，旨在提升游戏和软件的使用效果。对于 Yves 来说，这个项目可以帮助他在 Logic Labs 中探索如何通过改进用户体验来提高 AI 应用的互动性，同时也能为 TexTale 的电商平台提供灵感，提升用户在购物过程中的体验。作为一名创意设计从业者，他可以从中学习到用户体验设计的先进方法和思路。
